meta.title: perf: improve hashing performance for manual path
meta.body_summary: Refactor git_like_hash_file in turborepo-scm to stream file reads with an 8KB buffer instead of loading entire files into memory, claiming better hashing performance for large files.

perf_labels.notes: Cursor proposed streaming hash reads but provided no benchmark; maintainer CHANGES_REQUESTED BufReader + benchmark proof, then closed until real benchmarking exists.

change_scale_vs_repository.interpretation: At 230 changed lines, this is a small-to-medium edit by absolute volume, concentrated in one Rust source file plus a later-deleted summary doc. As a small-step performance PR it was closed—not merged—because maintainers required benchmark evidence that was never supplied.

review_details.concern_detail: chris-olszewski CHANGES_REQUESTED: use BufReader instead of a hand-rolled buffer loop and provide a benchmark to verify the performance claim; cited serde-json precedent where buffered reads can be slower than reading full files into memory.
review_details.performance_claim: Streaming 8KB-buffered reads reduce memory pressure and improve hashing performance versus read_to_end for large files.
review_details.rejection_signals: Maintainer anthonyshew closed the PR agreeing that real benchmarking is required before accepting the change.

capability_boundary.boundary_notes: Low-level Rust I/O micro-optimization in a mature monorepo tool; maintainers require benchmark proof and standard library patterns (BufReader) before merging performance claims.

detection_detail.code_reading: chris-olszewski reviewed the manual hashing implementation, questioned the hand-rolled buffer loop, and demanded benchmark validation referencing prior serde-json performance findings.

maintainer_practices.reproducibility_notes: PR body and a PERFORMANCE_IMPROVEMENT_SUMMARY.md claimed significant gains but included no benchmark numbers; the summary doc was deleted before close.
maintainer_practices.regression_detail: After CHANGES_REQUESTED, no benchmark was added; anthonyshew closed the PR explicitly until benchmarking exists.

core_change_summary.description: In crates/turborepo-scm/src/manual.rs, git_like_hash_file switched from read_to_end into a Vec to metadata-based blob header plus chunked 8KB reads while hashing; an accompanying PERFORMANCE_IMPROVEMENT_SUMMARY.md was added then removed.
core_change_summary.secondary_note: 195 of 230 changed lines were in the summary markdown (later deleted); the Rust implementation change is ~35 lines.
change_trajectory: Initial commit: streaming hash implementation and performance summary doc.
Follow-up: formatting and merge commits; summary doc deleted.
Review: CHANGES_REQUESTED for BufReader and benchmark; PR closed without benchmark.

review_signals.summary: Requested BufReader instead of hand-rolled buffering and a benchmark to verify the performance improvement; skeptical without measurement.
review_signals.action_taken: No benchmark added; PR eventually closed.

review_signals.summary: Asked to keep the existing symlink-handling comment in manual.rs even if the read method changes.
Agreed with reviewer; will close until real benchmarking that the team operates against is provided.
Vercel bot posted deployment preview metadata (unrelated to perf validation).

collaboration_trajectory: PR opened with streaming hash refactor and narrative performance summary.
Maintainer CHANGES_REQUESTED citing missing benchmark and preferring BufReader.
Author-side summary doc removed; no benchmark follow-up.
Maintainer closed PR explicitly pending benchmark evidence.
